definitions: An act or a an instance of balking; a check or hindrance.
A ridge of land left unplowed as a boundary line or through carelessness.
A roughly squared timber; a beam or rafter.
In baseball, an illegal motion by a pitcher while a runner is on base, entitling the runner to advance one base.
examples: The project hit a balk due to funding issues.
The old balk separated the two fields.
They used a heavy balk to support the roof.
The umpire called a balk, and the runner advanced to second.
